title: Renaissance Scholarship — Brainstorm + Draft Essays
html:
Sprawling Jan-26 brainstorm-and-draft doc for the Renaissance Scholarship. Two prompts: (1) the personal-challenge essay (alcoholism + forgiveness, abbreviated), (2) the self-introduction essay built around three threads — "lack of LatinX representation in business," entrepreneur idols (steve jobs · larry ellison · ray dalio), and being a first generation American from a Canyonville, Oregon restaurant family. Includes a third scrap on joe biden's "four historic crises at once" framing and Yahir's commitment to climate-aligned tech.

TL;DR

  • First explicit articulation of "there is a startling scarcity in Latino leadership in the field of business" as Yahir's mission frame — recurs as the spine of the Ford essays and the Al Forthan speech.
  • Names his entrepreneurial idols: steve jobs, larry ellison, ray dalio — "all starting at baseline struggle, progressing by struggling well through persistent effort."
  • The Canyonville, Oregon (population ~500) "decrepit Mexican restaurant" origin story enters the canon — Yahir's parents' first US restaurant, the reason they emigrated.
  • Quotes the casey neistat "do something you hate and you'll find yourself thinking about what you want to do" line — the dishwashing-while-dreaming-of-business anchor metaphor.
  • Cites joe biden's "four crises" frame (racial justice · economy · climate change · COVID-19) and stakes climate change as "the fight of our generation."
  • Concept threads: business as net positive · diplomacy as strength · problem solving as vocation · MeCHA presidency as proof-of-leadership.

Key quote

"I want to be an entrepreneur that my people, my community can look up to as an example. Nothing is more fulfilling than that goal to me."

Why it matters in the graph

The Jan-26 Renaissance doc is where the Latino-leadership-gap mission statement crystallizes. Every subsequent 2021 scholarship essay borrows from this doc. It's also the densest single source for Yahir's 2021 entrepreneur-idol pantheon (Jobs / Ellison / Dalio / Neistat implicit). The doc is half-finished — sentences cut mid-thought, MeCHA paragraph fully reused later — which is itself useful as a snapshot of the building-blocks phase of the essay machine.

Connect to: the 2021 06 27 Jann Renaissance Rejection and 2021 07 09 Hoff Renaissance notes show Yahir later declined this scholarship after winning Ford — the arc closes 6 months later.

Open questions

  • Why ray dalio specifically — did Yahir read Principles or pick him from podcasts?
  • The "decrepit Canyonville restaurant" detail is mentioned only here in 2021 — is it the same family restaurant that became gtanos, or an earlier one?

Connections

  • 2021 06 27 Jann Renaissance Rejection — the closing of this arc 5 months later.
  • 2021 07 09 Hoff Renaissance — second declination letter to a different administrator.
  • 2021 02 24 Ford Family Foundation Scholarship — borrows the same mission framing.
  • renaissance scholarship · latinx leadership gap · canyonville or · steve jobs · larry ellison · ray dalio · casey neistat · joe biden · mecha · climate change · first generation american
